response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable polyglot engineer with strong software craftsmanship practices, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. Java appears only as a footnote in his skills list with no substantive project evidence, Kotlin is entirely absent, and there is no experience with Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or any JVM enterprise framework. His primary expertise lies in TypeScript/Node.js and D language ecosystems. The one IDE-adjacent data point — writing C/C++ components for NI LabWindows — is peripheral and over a decade old.
strengths: Consistent software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, pair programming, trunk-based development, CI/CD) across multiple senior and staff-level roles
Brief IDE-adjacent experience at National Instruments contributing C/C++ components to NI LabWindows, a developer tool used by engineers globally
Demonstrated ability to own complex technical infrastructure end-to-end, including developer tooling and MCP-based agentic frameworks at Naver
concerns: Java is listed in skills but has no substantive project evidence; Kotlin is entirely absent from the resume
No experience whatsoever with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM-based enterprise framework
No background in IDE internals, language tooling, or JVM build tooling — the core technical focus of this role

key_factors.factor: IDE tooling exposure at NI LabWindows
